title: Human Resources Ministry will continue strengthening TVET ecosystem to meet global challenges, says Ramanan
description: PUTRAJAYA: The Human Resources Ministry will continue strengthening the Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) ecosystem to ensure the country has a highly skilled workforce capable of meeting global economic challenges that are being increasingly driven by artificial intelligence (AI), automation and industrial digitalisation, says Datuk Seri R. Ramanan.

title: AI solutions should be harnessed to address challenges in farm, health, education: NITI Aayog's Roy
description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) solutions should be developed to address challenges in sectors such as agriculture, healthcare and education, as enterprises solving real-world problems will find a market for their products, Anna Roy Principal Economic Advisor at NITI Aayog, told ANI today.

title: At a Tennessee Hospital, a Nurse Stole Fentanyl and AI Missed It, State Records Say
description: Sentri7, drug diversion software powered by artificial intelligence and used at hundreds of U.S. hospitals, did not catch a months-long string of fentanyl thefts in Tennessee in 2025, according to a state document.
content: About a year ago at Erlanger Baroness, the largest hospital in Chattanooga, anesthesia staff noticed that a nurse was slurring his words and struggling to stay awake while on duty in the surgery center, according to a Tennessee Board of Nursing conse... [9921 chars]

title: US envoy Sergio Gor to meet Piyush Goyal on trade deal Thursday
description: India and the United States are nearing a trade deal. US Ambassador Sergio Gor met with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal to finalize the agreement. Ninety-nine percent of the deal is reportedly settled. Both nations aim for mutually beneficial outcomes. Discussions also cover strategic sectors like artificial intelligence and semiconductors. India is also addressing US concerns regarding forced labor.
content:

title: DeepSeek Set to Raise $7.4 Bn in First Funding Round Backed by Tencent, CATL
description: Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) startup DeepSeek is preparing to raise about 50 billion yuan ($7.4 billion) in its first funding round, a deal that could value the company at between 350 billion yuan and 400 billion yuan ($52 billion-$59 billion), according to Reuters.
